name: Enchanted City Trail
name_original: La Ciudad Encantada – circular por el Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca
description: Explore the easy 1.6-mile circular hike through the Enchanted City in Serrania de Cuenca Natural Park, featuring unique rock formations.
region_intro: Hiking around Valdecabras offers access to a landscape shaped by unique geological formations within Spain's Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. The region is characterized by karst topography, featuring limestone and gypsum structures sculpted by natural erosion. Pine forests cover much of the terrain, providing varied environments for outdoor exploration. This area presents a range of hiking opportunities, from gentle paths through natural monuments to longer routes across the park's diverse features. Best hiking trails around Valdecabras The most popular hiking route is Enchanted City Trail, a 1.6 miles (2.5 km) trail that takes about 41 minutes to complete, leading through the whimsical rock formations of Ciudad Encantada. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de Uña y Ciudad Encantada — Serranía de Cuenca, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path. This route offers expansive views and traverses varied terrain within the natural park. Local hikers also love the Las Corralizas y Casa Tormo – circular from Cueva de los Morceguillos, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through forested areas and past rock formations, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Hiking around Valdecabras is defined by karst rock formations, dense pine forests, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Valdecabras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 2,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdecabras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valdecabras?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Valdecabras, ranging from easy strolls to more moderate excursions. The area offers a diverse network of paths through its unique karst landscape and pine forests.

Q: What kind of terrain and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Valdecabras?
A: Hiking in Valdecabras means exploring a landscape defined by its distinctive karst topography, featuring whimsical limestone and gypsum rock formations sculpted by natural erosion. You'll encounter dense pine forests, and some routes offer views of river gorges. Notable features include the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt; and the labyrinthine &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Callejones of Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there easy trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Valdecabras offers several easy trails perfect for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010714930/enchanted-city-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 2.5 km (1.6 miles) path through the unique rock formations. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012687296/las-corralizas-y-casa-tormo-circular-from-cueva-de-los-morceguillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Corralizas y Casa Tormo – circular from Cueva de los Morceguillos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.7 km (2.9 miles) and leads through forested areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Valdecabras area?
A: Yes, several routes in Valdecabras are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012687296/las-corralizas-y-casa-tormo-circular-from-cueva-de-los-morceguillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Corralizas y Casa Tormo – circular from Cueva de los Morceguillos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14708990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City – Tormo Alto loop from Ciudad Encantada&lt;/a&gt; is another great circular trail, allowing you to explore the famous rock formations.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to visit along the trails?
A: Beyond the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt;, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Callejones of Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;, which features similar fascinating rock formations. For breathtaking panoramic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas of the Júcar river gorges. The picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is also a beautiful spot for a scenic path.

Q: Is Valdecabras suitable for hiking with dogs?
A: Valdecabras is located within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within natural parks, provided they are kept on a leash and owners adhere to local regulations regarding waste disposal. It's always recommended to check specific rules for individual attractions like the Enchanted City before visiting.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Valdecabras?
A: The best time to hike in Valdecabras is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be quite hot, while winters can bring colder temperatures, though the unique geological formations are impressive year-round.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While many trails are easy, there are moderate options for those seeking a longer hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364709058/mirador-de-una-y-ciudad-encantada-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Uña y Ciudad Encantada — Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.2 km (9.4 miles) path that offers expansive views and traverses varied terrain within the natural park, providing a more extensive exploration of the area.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Many popular trailheads, such as those leading to the Enchanted City or the Ventano del Diablo, have designated parking areas nearby, making them easily accessible by car. It is advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Valdecabras?
A: The trails in Valdecabras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 570 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique and surreal rock formations, the well-maintained paths, and the overall natural beauty of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails around Valdecabras using public transport?
A: While some areas within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park might have limited public transport options, direct public transport to specific trailheads around Valdecabras is generally not widely available. Most visitors access the trails by private vehicle, which offers the most flexibility for exploring the region.

Q: What makes the geological formations in Valdecabras so unique?
A: The unique geological formations in Valdecabras, such as those found in the Enchanted City, are a result of millions of years of natural processes. The area was once part of the Tethys Sea 90 million years ago, leaving behind limestone and gypsum deposits. These softer rocks have since been sculpted by water, wind, and ice into the whimsical shapes and natural sculptures seen today.
tips: The Tormo Alto, a symbol of the Enchanted City of Cuenca.

The Enchanted City, declared a Natural Site of National Interest in 1929. This worldwide known geological phenomenon is made up of rock formations sculpted by the action of water, wind and ice, which, by eroding the rocks over centuries, have managed to model figures more than curious (human silhouettes, objects and animals) with a precision that is difficult to understand, until achieving a city that seems asleep under some mysterious spell.

faqs.question: What makes the Enchanted City trail unique?
faqs.answer: This trail is renowned for its extraordinary karst landscape, where millennia of erosion have sculpted limestone rocks into whimsical figures resembling animals, objects, and human silhouettes. It's like an open-air geological museum, offering a truly surreal and unforgettable experience.

faqs.question: What kind of rock formations can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter numerous fascinating formations, many of which have been given descriptive names. Look out for formations resembling "The Dog," "The Turtle," "The Elephant," "The Ships," and "The Face of Man." The site also features notable natural monuments like Tormo Alto and the main Enchanted City formations.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is considered an easy hike, making it suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels, including beginners and families. The paths are generally easily accessible and well-conditioned, allowing everyone to enjoy the unique scenery.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route located within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. There is dedicated parking available at the entrance to the Enchanted City site, which serves as the trailhead for this circular walk. For specific logistical details, it's advisable to check local information sources like visitacuenca.es.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Enchanted City?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enchanted City is a protected natural site and typically requires an entrance fee for access. It's best to check the official website or local tourism information for current pricing and operating hours before your visit.

faqs.question: What are the general regulations for hiking within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As part of a Natural Park, visitors are expected to respect the environment. This includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, and taking all litter with you. Specific regulations regarding activities like camping or fires should be checked with the park authorities or local tourism offices.

faqs.question: Is the Enchanted City trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Enchanted City is a popular tourist attraction, policies regarding dogs can vary. It's highly recommended to verify the current dog-friendly status and any specific leash rules directly with the site management or local tourism office before planning your visit with a pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Enchanted City?
faqs.answer: The Enchanted City can be visited year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful colors, making them ideal. Summer can be hot, so starting early is advisable, and winter can be magical with a dusting of snow, though some facilities might have reduced hours. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Even though it's an easy trail, it's always wise to be prepared. We recommend wearing comfortable clothing and appropriate footwear for walking outdoors. Bring sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as shaded areas can be limited. A camera is a must for capturing the unique rock formations!

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with sections of other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the [CIMA CU01] Ciudad Encantada * Valdecabras and [CIMA CU01] Ciudad Encantada * Salto de Villalba routes.

faqs.question: Are there any food or drink options near the Enchanted City?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are typically facilities such as cafes or restaurants located near the entrance of the Enchanted City site, offering refreshments and meals. It's a good idea to check their operating hours, especially during off-peak seasons.
